2605.25440.title: A Multi-Agent LLM Framework for Rating the Quality of Surgical Feedback
2605.25440.paper_summary: Verbal feedback delivered by attending surgeons in the operating room plays a critical formative role in resident trainee skill acquisition. Yet, assessing the quality of trainer feedback and its effectiveness in influencing trainee behavior during live surgery remains a challenge. Prior studies assessed feedback content relying on extensive manual annotation by expert human raters and focused on developing broad taxonomies that overlook the qualitative aspects of feedback delivery such as clarity or urgency. Limited existing automated methods, including keyword analysis and topic modeling, also fail to capture these nuanced aspects. We introduce a two-stage LLM-based framework that discovers interpretable feedback quality criteria grounded in the context of surgical training. Our method uses multi-agent prompting and surgical domain knowledge injection to discover a small set of human interpretable scoring criteria (e.g., Encouraging, Urgent, Clear). These criteria are then used to automatically score live surgical feedback via an LLM-as-a-judge approach. Evaluation on 4.2k trainer feedback instances demonstrates that our AI-discovered criteria outperform prior content-based frameworks in predicting feedback effectiveness, including observed trainee behavioral adjustments and trainer approval. This work advances scalable, human-aligned assessment of communication quality in the operating room and provides a foundation for improving surgical teaching practices.
2605.25440.paper_summary_zh: 口頭反饋由手術室內的主治醫生提供，對住院醫師的技能獲得起著關鍵的形成性作用。<lb/>然而，評估培訓者反饋的質量及其在實時手術中影響受訓者行為的有效性仍然是一個挑戰。<lb/>先前的研究依賴專家人類評估者的廣泛手動標註來評估反饋內容，並專注於開發廣泛的分類法，忽略了反饋傳遞的質量方面，如清晰度或緊急性。<lb/>現有的自動化方法有限，包括關鍵字分析和主題建模，也未能捕捉這些微妙的方面。<lb/>我們介紹了一個基於LLM的兩階段框架，旨在發現基於外科訓練背景的可解釋反饋質量標準。<lb/>我們的方法使用多代理提示和外科領域知識注入來發現一小組人類可解釋的評分標準（例如，鼓勵、緊急、清晰）。<lb/>這些標準隨後用於通過LLM作為評判者的方法自動評分實時手術反饋。<lb/>對4200個培訓者反饋實例的評估顯示，我們AI發現的標準在預測反饋有效性方面超越了先前基於內容的框架，包括觀察到的受訓者行為調整和培訓者的批准。<lb/>這項工作推進了可擴展的、與人類對齊的手術室溝通質量評估，並為改善外科教學實踐提供了基礎。
2605.25440.author: Rafal Kocielnik et.al.
2605.25440.authors: Rafal Kocielnik, J. Everett Knudsen, Steven Y. Cen, Jasmine Lin, Cherine H. Yang, Atharva Deo, Ujjwal Pasupulety, Peter Wager, Anima Anandkumar, Andrew J. Hung
